summary: Matched a small block of FtFox functions by moving four routines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s into src/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.c, adding the C object to e_files.mk, and exposing the minimal fighter field and external function prototypes needed for compilation. The PR body says these were "a few simple functions" and explicitly stopped before func_800E576C because it was still unresolved.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementations for func_800E55A8, func_800E5688, func_800E56D0, and func_800E5718 from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s.
Added C implementations of func_800E55A8, func_800E5688, func_800E56D0, and func_800E5718 to src/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.c.
Left func_800E576C in assembly; the PR body states work stopped there because it stumped the author.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk so the new C translation unit participates in the build.
Added ftfox.h prototypes for the newly matched FtFox functions.
Promoted several previously unknown declarations in include/functions.h to typed prototypes: func_80070C48, func_80070CC4, func_80070FB4, func_8026B2B4, and func_8026B320.
Extended Fighter struct layout in src/melee/ft/fighter.h by splitting filler around offset 0x1974 and naming that field x1974_heldItem.
smart_moves: Kept the scope small and stopped at func_800E576C instead of forcing a speculative match for a function the author did not understand.
Introduced only the Fighter field needed by the matched code, x1974_heldItem at offset 0x1974, preserving surrounding layout with adjusted filler arrays.
Used direct HSD_GObj user_data access to recover the Fighter pointer, matching the assembly pattern that loads gobj->0x2C and then the held-item field.
Recovered the branch structure of func_800E55A8 as a simple switch over func_8026B320(ft->x1974_heldItem), mapping item-state values 1 through 4 to func_80070FB4 third arguments 1, 0, 2, and 3.
Converted repeated item-check guard patterns into compact C conditionals: call func_8026B2B4 on the held item and only perform follow-up fighter/item animation calls when it returns 0.
decomp_lessons: For fighter routines, a load from gobj->user_data followed by access to offset 0x1974 can indicate the fighter's held-item pointer; this PR used the provisional name x1974_heldItem.
Simple wrapper functions often match cleanly as guarded calls around unknown engine helpers when prototypes are made precise enough for argument and return types.
A dense chain of cmpwi branches in assembly may match naturally as a C switch when the cases are small integer constants and default behavior is no-op.
When matching a subset of a file, remove only the assembly functions that are replaced and leave the next unresolved symbol in asm to avoid overreaching.
Updating the build list is part of asm-to-C migration: adding the new object to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED was required for the C implementation to replace the removed assembly.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as func_800E55A8, func_80070FB4, and func_8026B320.
Newly identified struct fields used offset-prefixed provisional naming, e.g. x1974_heldItem.
Unknown filler arrays in Fighter retained data_filler_N names and were renumbered after splitting the layout around the new field.
Function arguments used generic names such as gobj and arg1 where semantic names were not yet evident.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the assembly load sequence lwz r31, 0x2c(r3); lwz r3, 0x1974(r31) into Fighter* ft = gobj->user_data; ft->x1974_heldItem.
Matched func_800E55A8 by preserving the guard func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem) == 0 before the switch and optional func_80070C48(gobj, 1) call.
Matched func_800E5688 and func_800E56D0 as small guarded wrappers around func_80070CC4(gobj, 1) and func_80070C48(gobj, 1), respectively.
Matched func_800E5718 as an unconditional func_80070FB4(gobj, 1, -1) followed by an optional func_80070CC4(gobj, 1) when arg1 is nonzero.
Added typed prototypes returning s32 for func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320 so comparisons against integer return values would compile naturally.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that match func_800E576C in ftfox.s.
Search for later renames or type refinements of Fighter::x1974_heldItem.
Search for later semantic names for func_80070C48, func_80070CC4, func_80070FB4, func_8026B2B4, and func_8026B320.
Compare other fighter character files for the same held-item guard pattern around func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320.
